topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed a monotonous tune, and Elias felt his eyelids growing heavy, weighted down by the afternoon slump. He was staring blankly at the spreadsheet, the numbers swimming together into an indistinct blur. Then, a notification popped up. A salary comparison report, accidentally sent to his inbox. His breath hitched. The figures detailed the compensation of a junior analyst, a colleague he barely acknowledged. A slow, sinking feeling began to spread through his chest. He blinked hard, trying to clear the haze, but the reality of the discrepancy remained stubbornly sharp. He leaned back in his chair, the cheap plastic groaning under his weight, and let his head droop until his chin rested on his chest, the world starting to narrow to just the rhythmic throb behind his eyes.

[story 2]

Maya rubbed her temples, the pressure behind her eyes building with every passing minute. The afternoon sun, slanting through the blinds, felt warm on her forehead, a dull, insistent warmth that made focusing on the quarterly reports an exercise in futility. She clicked on the shared payroll document, intending only to verify her own entry, but the screen glowed with stark, undeniable figures. The disparity was enormous. A quiet, hollow sound escaped her lips. She felt a strange sluggishness creeping into her limbs, a heavy languor that made lifting her hand feel like moving through thick syrup. She closed her eyes for a moment, letting the quiet weight of the realization settle over her, a profound exhaustion washing over her senses.

[story 3]

David kept blinking, the edges of his vision softening, the sharp lines of the monitor blurring into watercolor washes. He shifted in his ergonomic chair, finding the position suddenly too accommodating, too inviting. He pulled up the internal compensation matrix, a deliberate action to ground himself. The data presented itself, and a cold knot tightened in his stomach. The junior staff member’s earnings dwarfed his own by a significant margin. A profound wave of inertia washed over him; the effort required to process this imbalance felt monumental. He leaned his head against the cool back of his monitor, a soft, involuntary sigh escaping him as his awareness began to drift toward the edge of consciousness.
